intro: Ophrys quadriloba, also known as the Four-leafed Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique four-lobed leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ophrys quadriloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ophrys quadriloba is often made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
